### Post by H.om on 2009-05-29
The file named "root" in the location "/var/spool/cron/crontabs"
has contents as follows.
[FONT="Tahoma"]
# DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE - edit the master and reinstall.
# (/tmp/crontab.zZ01QN/crontab installed on Fri May 29 19:24:49 2009)
# (Cron version -- $Id: crontab.c,v 2.13 1994/01/17 03:20:37 vixie Exp $)
# 5,10,15,20,25,30,35,40,45,50,55,60 * * * *   rm /home/harry/Desktop/harry
[/FONT]

I want the command "rm /home/harry/Desktop/harry" to run every 5 min.

But it does not work.
When I checked ,I found that cron daemon is not running .How can I run crond


"I checked this by using the command "ps aux |grep crond".
somone pls help.

---

### Post by kpkeerthi on 2009-05-29
Check if cron is disabled under System -> Administration -> Services

To start crond manually,
```
sudo /etc/init.d/cron start
```

---

Sorry, I misread his post. Try this.

```

```
